question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/responseCacheHandler.js -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: cacheKeyGenerator()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a URL whose MD5 hash collides with a legitimate block-query cache key, causing the cache to serve a stale block response for 500%+ of the average block time, effectively freezing visible transaction state for all consumers of that endpoint?
